name: Pamplona - Irurita – return via Belate pass and Eugiko urtegia reservoir
name_original: Pamplona - Irurita – vuelta por puerto de Belate y embalse Eugiko urtegia
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Huarte/Uharte are set within the diverse landscapes of Navarra, Spain, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region features verdant mountains, fertile valleys, and rolling plains, with access to routes traversing the foothills of the Pyrenees. Rivers like the Arga and Irati have shaped picturesque gorges, providing opportunities for cycling along greenways. This area provides a network of routes suitable for different skill levels, from challenging climbs to leisurely rides. Best road cycling routes around Huarte/Uharte The most popular road cycling route is Puerto de Erro – return from Huarte via Zubiri (EuroVelo 3), a 35.5 miles (57.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 34 minutes to complete. This route offers a moderate challenge with significant elevation changes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Clasica Pamplona - Zubiri, an easy 19.7 miles (31.7 km) path. This route follows a section of the historic Way of St. James, often utilizing secondary roads. Local road cyclists also love the Valle de Aranguren – return via Ardanaz, a 33.3 miles (53.6 km) trail leading through fertile valleys and rural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 51 minutes. Road cycling around Huarte/Uharte is defined by Pyrenean foothills, river valleys, and greenways. The extensive network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Huarte/Uharte are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 500 reviews. More than 20,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Huarte/Uharte's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Huarte/Uharte?
A: There are over 200 road cycling routes around Huarte/Uharte, catering to various skill levels. This includes approximately 19 easy routes, over 100 moderate routes, and around 90 difficult routes for experienced cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Huarte/Uharte?
A: The region around Huarte/Uharte offers a diverse range of terrain for road cyclists. You can expect verdant mountains, fertile valleys, and rolling plains. Routes often traverse the foothills of the Pyrenees, providing challenging climbs, while others follow picturesque river valleys like the Arga and Irati, sometimes utilizing greenways converted from old railway lines.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Huarte/Uharte?
A: Yes, there are several routes suitable for beginners or families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984940826/clasica-pamplona-zubiri" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Clasica Pamplona - Zubiri&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 19.7-mile path that follows a section of the historic Way of St. James, often on secondary roads with less traffic. The region offers around 19 easy routes in total.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists around Huarte/Uharte?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984948834/pamplona-irurita-return-via-belate-pass-and-eugiko-urtegia-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pamplona - Irurita – return via Belate pass and Eugiko urtegia reservoir&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gains and longer distances. The region features approximately 90 difficult routes, including those traversing passes like Lizarraga and Urbasa, providing rewarding climbs and stunning views.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I see while road cycling near Huarte/Uharte?
A: Many routes offer opportunities to explore historical and cultural sites. You can incorporate visits to attractions in and around Pamplona, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4396525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pamplona Cathedral and the Citadel&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4627298" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Citadel of Pamplona&lt;/a&gt;. The Magdalena Bridge, a medieval structure, is also notable as it marks an entrance to the Camino de Santiago.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available from Huarte/Uharte?
A: Yes, you can find circular road cycling routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10524818" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Badostáin – View of Ardanaz loop from Burlada / Burlata&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic loop through the area. Many routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: Do any routes connect to larger cycling networks like EuroVelo?
A: Yes, Huarte/Uharte is strategically located near important EuroVelo routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986655015/puerto-de-erro-return-from-huarte-via-zubiri-eurovelo-3" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Erro – return from Huarte via Zubiri (EuroVelo 3)&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a route that utilizes the EuroVelo 3, known as the "Pilgrims' Route." The EuroVelo 1 and 3 converge from Pamplona, offering long-distance journey options with historical and natural significance. For more information on EuroVelo 1 in Navarra, you can visit &lt;a href="https://www.visitnavarra.es/en/bike-and-mtb/eurovelo-1-in-navarra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitnavarra.es&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Huarte/Uharte?
A: The region is generally excellent for outdoor activities, including road cycling, during spring and autumn when temperatures are mild and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, though some routes might be more exposed to heat. Winter conditions can vary, with higher elevations potentially experiencing colder temperatures or snow.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Huarte/Uharte?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 500 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, ranging from challenging Pyrenean foothills to serene river valleys, and the extensive network of routes suitable for all abilities. The opportunity to combine cycling with visits to historical sites also receives positive feedback.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes in Huarte/Uharte?
A: Many routes offer stunning vistas. You can find viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2867082" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Monte Ezkaba&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2853366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Balsa de Ezkotitz&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5577871" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Mount Santa Cruz or Zambeta&lt;/a&gt;, providing rewarding panoramas of the surrounding natural beauty.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Huarte/Uharte?
A: Huarte/Uharte and nearby towns often have public parking areas available. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the route details on komoot, as many start points are located near accessible parking. Pamplona, being a larger city nearby, also offers various parking options from which many routes can be accessed.
tips: Very popular road port among cyclists in the area, with a good climb coming from Pamplona up to 847 meters above sea level.
Highway port at 984 masl, very popular in the area since you can get here both by road and by paths, either by mtb or on foot. A very quiet place with very beautiful panoramic views.

The waters of the Arga river were retained by means of a damming in 1973, to supply water to the population of Pamplona and its region.

The landscape of the town of Eugi changed forever, since the forestry house, the oven, the bakery and the mill of the town were left under the waters of the swamp.
